spec_match.rationale: Builds a real `new VideoFrame(canvas, { rotation, flip })` and lets the renderer use the resulting frame, exactly the new constructor surface the spec adds.

interactive.rationale: Select for rotation, checkbox for flip, button to construct the frame, plus a feature-detect probe — the painted frame on the right updates as the controls change.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index promises `Construct a real VideoFrame from a canvas with {rotation, flip} metadata` and the painted result rotates/mirrors automatically — that is what ships.

edge_cases.rationale: Demonstrates the 4×2 rotation/flip combos when the user steps through, but never explores invalid rotation (e.g. 45) error path, the case where `rotation` is absent (default 0), or the visible difference between `flip: true` and a 180° rotation.

references.rationale: `see also` cites https://chromestatus.com/feature/5128855343595520 and the WebCodecs spec VideoFrame interface.

design_system.rationale: Mostly uses CSS variables, but inline styles include raw hex (`#fdfcf8`, `#0022ff`, `#d11a2a`, `#0a8f5c`, `#f4f3ee`) that bypass the variable set defined in `public/styles.css`. No button-color/background mismatch detected.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex colours with design-system variables
openQuestions.detail: Inline styles use `#fdfcf8`, `#0022ff`, `#d11a2a`, `#0a8f5c`, `#f4f3ee`. CLAUDE.md invariant #5 mandates only the variables defined in `public/styles.css` (`--bg-ivory/paper/stone`, `--text-*`, `--accent-blue/emerald/rose`). Swap each hex for its closest variable so the page tracks the rest of the design system.

openQuestions.title: Show the invalid-rotation error path
openQuestions.detail: The spec accepts rotation as multiples of 90; non-multiples should throw / coerce. Add a small `try invalid (45)` button that attempts `new VideoFrame(canvas, { rotation: 45 })` and surfaces the resulting error, so the page documents the API's input-validation contract.

openQuestions.title: Distinguish flip from 180° rotation visually
openQuestions.detail: Visually `rotation:180` and `flip:true` can look similar on a symmetric source canvas. Either change the source canvas to be asymmetric (an arrow or `F`-shape) or add a small annotation comparing the two transforms so the difference is unambiguous.

summary: Solid headline demo of the new VideoFrame metadata constructor — uses the real API, has a working feature probe, and links the chromestatus / spec. Two cleanups: swap inline hex for variables, and ensure the source canvas is asymmetric so `flip` vs `rotation:180` is visually distinguishable.
